reviews.review_description: This game is, potentially, an entertaining addition to my VR workout. However, I could find no accessibility options. The first exercise it asked me to do was a full-on side plank with a bad back and hips. The next time I tried it asked me to do burpees. I was unable to get it to acknowledge that I was in the starting position. (Not that I would be able to do a proper burpee.) I was unable to find a beginner mode or any way to allow me to skip an exercise.  The "custom" option did not work properly either so all I have been able to do is the level 1 run portion of the game. I will certainly keep checking back for improvements, but, as is, the game is not much good to me. I hope they are still working on this and one day it will be worth the money I spent.
For some reason I need to jump instead of running… any suggestions?

It’s certainly more heart thumping than all the other fitness apps out there on Oculus but it has its limitations too.  The running in place did not work for me.  The VR headset kept bouncing up and down.  It’s just too heavy to stay glued to your face during running.  I had to hold it with one hand.  I like that it incorporates the burpees and sit-ups among other exercises.  Shooting at the alien ship with bare hands was awkward.  It felt like aiming was a bit off.  Overall ok but I can’t get passed the bopping of the headset during running.

developer_response.body:
Hi ! Thank you for your feedback ! 🙂
The Run phases are indeed not ideal with such a front heavy device.
To prevent the headset from bouncing, you can try tightening the strap or getting a rigid one. Hope this helps !
If you have any suggestion that you think could fix this issue (or improve the shooting !), I'd be glad to hear it ! 🙂 (via Discord !)

Update !
Since the v1.0d, you can do squats instead of running in the Run phases !

reviews.review_title: Seems like it will be good
reviews.review_description:
I like the concept, even though I don't like exercise.  I was excited to try it similar to how Ring Fit Adventures works with the Switch.

The running seemed awkward at first, but manageable.  You have to exaggerate the running.  I jumped my first time through because I didn't know how else to do it.

The aiming for shooting is really off though.  I was trying to recalibrate my hand gestures to see if I could change it.  Maybe a manual projection adjustment would be ideal for those parts.

I also know I'm out of shape, but the abs (crunches or sit up's.  I couldn't tell which) exercise was hard to get high enough to register.

But I'm excited for the development.  I think these are all relatively minor gripes and (hopefully) relatively easy changes.  I know it's meant to be an exercise game, and it was really cool.  Especially with the hand tracking to feel that but more immersive.

Best wishes on the development.  I'll be checking in periodically!

developer_response.body:
Hi ! Thank you so much for your feedback ! ✨

You shouldn't have to actually jump during the Run phase, neither should any step during the exercises be hard to reach. Since all of those are closely related to your Oculus Guardian's floor height, can you check if it's correctly configured ?
(since the v1.0d update, you can do squats instead of running !)

About the aiming for shooting, I don't quite understand what you mean by manual projection but I'd love to hear about it more in detail ! (via Discord !)
If the running/abs issue still persists, please DM me so we can troubleshoot it together. Thanks !

reviews.review_description: I have the same problem as the other user, not able to pass the first screen with hand gesture.

developer_response.body:
Hi, please DM me on Discord or join the server so we can troubleshoot this issue together. If you don't have Discord, please email me at wasplay@sfr.fr
Sorry about it ! 😔

reviews.review_title: Worth it, but could be even better.
reviews.review_description:
I have done two workouts so far.  What I like is the gamification of a workout that has pretty clear/intuitive instructions for doing the task.  For me, a pretty active 54 year-old, the tasks are challenging but achievable and it's clear to me that it will challenge me more when I'm ready.   My likes for this game are enough to keep me going, unless I find another app that does the same thing but looks and functions better.   Great job so far.

Have said that, I have some feedback with hopes of getting some things that would improve my experience:
1.  The graphics need some professional overhaul.  It gives me the feel of 90s era web sites where people have no concept of visual hierarchy, color schemes, and that every design choice must add value (think "less is more").  I think a graphic designer could spend an hour suggesting improvements.  For example, using a different border radius for the buttons than the windows creates visual chaos.  Use something else if you want someone to notice a button.   Examples: Wooden platform fights with the other design elements, red font for end-of-task summary,   font choice for the enemy dialog.  Pick  a consistent color scheme and stick with it.  Do some research on the font families that are appropriate for dialog, menus, etc.
2.  The movement of the platform and clouds makes me sick to my stomach at game startup.  It causes vertigo.  Until we have something else to focus on, I suggest you don't do that or reduce the effects.  I'm curious if anyone else experiences that.  I use teleport in other games (e.g., walkabout minigolf) and sometimes feel a little queasy, but this games startup is nauseating.
3.  The duck-walk game puts some targets outside my defined boundary.  I'm running into furniture.  
4.  When I started the game the 2nd day, I got a "go back to you play area" arrow even though I was in it.  I had to restart the game.
5.  Need small HUD when forced to look away (e.g., stretching) showing pose and progress bar.

This is a hidden gem. I actually feel like I got a good work out. I was impressed by how it tracked push up and squats. I would not be surprised if this game ends up on the official oculus store. If you are on the fence, just go ahead and buy it, it's only $3.99.  you won't regret it.😎👍

Purchased and installed game onto Quest 2 yesterday and only got the logo then a black screen with music playing. Rebooted headset, reopened VRIT and made it to the main screen with tiles on floor in the clouds and music playing, but it was totally unresponsive, couldn't click on anything, let alone exercise.  The link to program website is a chat forum where I posted my concern and questions of how to get program to respond. My Quest 2 is only a few months old and I only have a few games uploaded to it. It was only $3.99 but that's really irrelevant when you expect the darn thing to work.

developer_response.body:
Hi, sorry to hear you're having issues with the game.
Please DM me on Discord (Gabwiel#0706) or join the server so we can troubleshoot this issue together. If you don't have Discord, please email me at wasplay@sfr.fr
Really sorry about it ! 😔

reviews.review_title: Short fitness routine with hand tracking
reviews.review_description:
I've started to use this as an additional little daily routine, because it's surprisingly fun and addicting. Since it uses hand tracking, you can easily change positions during the workout session. 
Personally I wished the focus would be less on running and more on push ups etc. I have no idea what will be available in the "shop" and if you can use the crystals for it.

developer_response.body:
Hi ! Thank you so much for your feedback :)

About the running phases, could we please talk about it on the Discord server ? I agree with you but I'm not sure what I could do to change it.

The shop will include cosmetics, such as little props and new surfaces for your tiny island. Stay tuned !

reviews.review_description: This game is great to get you starting to exercise daily with a fun game hands free no controllers. It's even better after the game to make your own routine to really challenge your own personal limits!!! I fully support this developer and hope he will continue to upgrade the game as technology advances as well. THANK YOU FOR THIS!!!😃😃😃
This is how vr should be pushups, situps, squats, burpees also warmup and stretches excellent. 5 stars and thankyou for being an active developer.

first try...so fun...will go back tomorrow ...need to work on 5 situps but did the push ups! yaaay!

tip: start hand tracking prior to opening app 😀
This game is so good. It's fun and engaging and I really hope the developer adds to it or makes another similar game. Thank you for making VRit.

developer_response.body:
Glad you like it! 💪😊
I try to add new content/changes as soon as I have some free time (which is not often). But feel free to contact me through Discord or email if you have any suggestion !

reviews.review_description:
This is one of my favorite workout games. It can help make exercise more entertaining ,motivating and interesting. Vrit offers a solid variety of different workouts and gradually becomes more challenging to test your limits.
The workouts are hands free and can help you burn calories and build muscle. I highly recommend it. The price is worth it. The game is well made.
No subscription either. Imagine that and to top it all off you could achieve a better workout in Vrit in the comfort of your home than going to a gym. 
This game is so underrated.

developer_response.body:
Glad you like it! 😊💪 However VRIT isn't meant to replace going to the gym, it's meant to be complementary to whatever sport you do next to it.
Feel free to contact me through Discord or email if you have any suggestion !

reviews.review_description:
There are several games offered in VR that claim to be fitness oriented, but most of them are rhythm games that offer a little bit of cardio workout. The games that can offer a real workout are very few and usually require a subscription. VRIT however is a shining exception, where you get a unique experience that makes exercising fun at a very low price. Well done to the developer and I hope this gets the recognition it deserves so that more titles with higher budget get produced in the future following a similar formula.
Note for the 1 star reviews, both of which gave that rating because they experienced issues with hand tracking / gestures. I see the dev is willing to support you via discord so you shall try that and resolve your issue. I really do not believe that your issue is the game's fault so giving that rating seems very unfair to my eyes. I suggest you reconsider :).

Hi there love the game great idea but i need a bit more info im stuck on the crouch move in day 4 PLEASE give me more info on what i need to do so i crouch and theres a green triangle than what do i do?

developer_response.body:
Hi ! You have to crouch so your head is between the two red dots layers, then reach the green arrows ! (crouched walk as the title suggests 🙂)
Everything is explained before each exercise, be sure to read carefully before starting the exercise, and if you did, I'm really sorry for the confusion caused !

Have fun ✨

Update !
If you still have issues with the Crouched Walking phase, you can now disable it completely in the main menu !

reviews.review_title: Changes the game for VR fitness
reviews.review_description:
I really don't understand why no one else has done this. Every fitness game just focuses on a lot of arm movement and some squats. This game has actual exercises. Burpees, crunches, running, squats, pushups...
This is one I'll probably be playing every day
One of the best fitness apps for quest. Very high replayability, and at 4.99 you’re not losing much if you don’t like it.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review ! 💪✨
Feel free to contact me if you have any suggestion !

reviews.review_description:
This app is very good for workouts. The daily challenge is great with a fun story but the best part at this point seems to be where you can build your own workouts and the game has its way of making sure you do full reps.

The only negatives I have at this point is mainly with the forced hand tracking. Using the controllers in the menus can make things much easier. It is hard to get the hang of how to change the workout/rep amount.

Overall, Great Game 4/5

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback ! 💪✨
About the forced hand-tracking, I don't plan to add controller support but the next update should improve the hand-tracking accuracy.

reviews.review_title: Excellent! Makes exercising fun and easy
reviews.review_description: Perfect for a quick sweat!!
